title: MOSFET Linear-Mode Constant-Current Electronic Load
content: A constant-current (CC) electronic load uses an N-MOSFET biased in its linear (ohmic-to-saturation) region by a feedback op-amp. The op-amp compares a shunt-resistor voltage (I_set × R_shunt) against a setpoint voltage, adjusting the gate to maintain I_drain = V_setpoint / R_shunt.
extended_content: Design: op-amp (LM358 or TL071) non-inverting input = setpoint voltage from DAC or potentiometer; inverting input = V_shunt = I_drain × R_shunt. Op-amp output drives MOSFET gate. Feedback loop maintains I_drain constant as V_drain (DUT voltage) varies. Power dissipation P = V_drain × I_set — MOSFET must be mounted on a heatsink; thermal resistance θ_JA must satisfy T_junction = T_ambient + P × θ_JA ≤ T_j_max (typically 150 °C). IRFP250N (200 V, 30 A) is commonly used. Accuracy: limited by op-amp offset voltage and R_shunt tolerance (1 % metal film). Current range: 0–5 A typical for bench-top DIY load; 0.1 Ω shunt gives 0–500 mV at full scale. DAC (MCP4725 I²C, 12-bit) allows digital setpoint control from MCU. Measurement: INA219 or current shunt + ADC reads actual current for display. Safety: include series diode on DUT+ to prevent reverse polarity; TVS diode at MOSFET drain for inductive load protection.
formula: I_load = V_setpoint / R_shunt;  P_MOSFET = V_DUT × I_load
units: I_load: A, V_setpoint: V, R_shunt: Ω, P: W
source: Horowitz & Hill, The Art of Electronics, 3rd ed.; Analog Devices App Note AN-531
